rulings.Case Title: BVH, LLC VS. S.F. HOTEL MASTER TENANT, LLC, DBA HOTEL PROPER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Date, Time And Place Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER BVH, LLC'S Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Petitioner BVH, LLC seeks to confirm the arbitration award entered on March 4, 2025, and seek a final judgment based on the award. A party may seek a court judgment confirming an arbitration or appraisal award by filing and serving a petition at least 10 days, but no more than four years after the award is served. (CCP, sec. 1288 and 1288.4) The court must confirm the award as made, unless it corrects or vacates the award, or dismisses the proceeding. (Id. sec. 1286.) If the award is confirmed, an enforceable judgment is entered with the same force and effect as a judgment in a civil action. (Id., sec. 1287.4.) Petitioner has filed a proof of service showing service on March 20, 2024, ten days after the recited service date of March 10. The petition contains the arbitration agreement, the name of the arbitrator, and a copy of the award. The court grants the unopposed petition to confirm the award. Petitioner shall prepare a proposed judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. JOSEPH GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Civil Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S Motion To Lift Stay Of Civil Case On May 2, 2022, this court stayed the proceedings in this case until further order of the court. The stay was apparently in view of criminal proceedings involving the defendant. The charges have proceeded to judgment. Plaintiff now seeks to lift the stay, and the motion is unopposed. The court grants the motion and lifts the stay of proceedings. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2025, line 3, DEFENDANT ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT (tentative ruling Part 1 of 2) Defendant Robert Bosch Semiconductor LLC (Bosch)'s motion to strike a plea for special damages from Pakal Technologies, Inc. (Pakal)'s second amended complaint (SAC) is denied for the reasons stated herein. According to the SAC, Bosch and its predecessor TSI Semiconductors (TSI) breached a semiconductor manufacturing contract with Pakal that not only deprived Pakal of its lost profits and other direct benefits of the contract but also damaged the trajectory of its nascent business in various ways. (SAC para. 5.) Pakal seeks to recover special damages: its lost profits on orders that it might have obtained had TSI and Bosch performed (SAC para. 19) and the cost of its emergency funding (SAC para. 39). (The parties dispute whether the former is better characterized as general damages. Because the court denies the motion to strike even under the heightened pleading standard for special damages, it does not resolve this dispute.) "Unlike general damages, special damages are those losses that do not arise directly and inevitably from any similar breach of any similar agreement. Instead, they are secondary or derivative losses arising from circumstances that are particular to the contract or to the parties. Special damages are recoverable if the special or particular circumstances from which they arise were actually communicated to or known by the breaching party (a subjective test) or were matters of which the breaching party should have been aware at the time of contracting (an objective test)." (Schellinger Brothers v. Cotter (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 984, 1010 [internal quotation and paragraph marks and citations omitted].) Special damages must be pleaded with particularity: "A defendant cannot be presumed to be aware of the special damage resulting from his act, and therefore, in order to prevent a surprise on him, this sort of damage must be specially set forth in the complaint, or the plaintiff will not be permitted to give evidence of it. (8 Cal.Jur. p. 889, sec. 127.) . . . The facts as to special damages must be stated with particularity. The amount of such damages must be stated with particularity. The amount of such damages must be given, and the means of occasioning them must be set forth." (Shook v. Pearson (1950) 99 Cal.App.2d 348, 351-352.) The court previously granted Bosch's motion to strike; the SAC adds new facts in response. The court now concludes that Pakal's pleading is sufficient. The particularity requirement serves a notice function but there is no indication in the cases that it is intended to weed out claims on their merits at the pleading stage. Pakal's description is sufficient to provide notice of what is claimed and thus what the parties' discovery with respect to special damages should focus on. Pakal pleads amounts of damages with as much specificity as is available. That Bosch believes Pakal's damages allegations are too speculative or remote from the contract to support relief is a merits determination that may be made at summary judgment or trial. (end of tentative ruling part 1, see part 2) = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2025, line 3, DEFENDANT ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2) Bosch also contends that Pakal's pleading is insufficient as to Bosch's knowledge of these contract damages. Pakal pleads that TMI and Bosch were aware of Pakal's vulnerability in light of their knowledge of the industry (SAC para. 15), that it informed TMI of facts related to special damages (SAC paras. 18-20), and that it informed Bosch of similar facts after Bosch announced its intent to breach (SAC para. 30). This pleading is sufficient to inform Bosch of the basis for Pakal's contention that either the subjective or objective test for knowledge is met. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. FOURSTAR GROUP INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2025, line 5, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
